game_name: Super Street Fighter II: The New Challengers

element_introduction_order.first_appears: From Street Fighter II series baseline
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player selects any of 12 established fighters; mechanics refined from prior versions

element_introduction_order.element: Four new characters (Cammy, T. Hawk, Dee Jay, Fei Long)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New character models and balance adjustments; playable immediately in arcade/versus modes
Tracks hits, first attacks, reversals, recoveries; bonus points awarded during gameplay
Cosmetic choice; original color plus Champion Edition, Hyper Fighting, and five new schemes
SNES version includes tournament mode; requires mastery of character selection and sequential match play

element_introduction_order.element: Speed setting adjustment (reduced from Hyper Fighting back to Champion Edition pace)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Accessible from options; affects frame data and reaction timing requirements

element_introduction_order.element: New special techniques (Fire Hadōken, flaming Shoryuken, Atomic Buster, Devil Reverse)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Refinement of existing character movesets; balanced adjustments to existing mechanics

element_introduction_order.element: Eight random opponents, then four Grand Masters (Balrog, Vega, Sagat, M. Bison)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: CPU difficulty escalates; final four opponents represent peak challenge difficulty

typical_tension_triggers: Grand Masters introduction in arcade mode
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Round-based tension cycle: each match represents discrete tension arc with buildup during fight, relief on victory, then immediate reset for next opponent. Tournament mode compounds tension through sequential matches without breaks between rounds.

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Turbo speed setting originally required button code (Left, Down, Down+Right, L at title screen); now accessible via menu

difficulty_curve.early_game: Eight random CPU opponents with balanced difficulty; player can select any of 16 characters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progression toward Grand Masters tier; repeated matches in Tournament Battle mode increase cumulative difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: Grand Masters phase: Balrog, Vega, Sagat, M. Bison as final four opponents representing peak CPU challenge
notable_spikes: Transition from random eight opponents to Grand Masters tier
Tournament Battle mode: sequential elimination matches without recovery breaks
Speed settings: Champion Edition pace baseline, with turbo/slower options affecting reaction time requirements

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight randomly-selected opponents from full 16-character roster, followed by fixed four Grand Masters (Balrog, Vega, Sagat, M. Bison). Single-elimination format.

Eight-player single-elimination tournament across four networked arcade cabinets. Three sets: initial eliminations (4 simultaneous matches), semifinals, finals. Players re-arranged by outcome between sets.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tournament Battle (arcade exclusive, included in SNES)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each character has associated stage background. New graphics on all stages and character portraits in this version. Game includes 16 playable stage locations.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ryu's stage (used in promotional Cammy vs. Fei-Long example)

notable_level_patterns.description: SNES exclusive game modes providing alternative progression structures beyond traditional arcade ladder.

sources_used: tcrf - Super Street Fighter II: The New Challengers (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
